title: Biden wins, Pfizer vaccine, markets rip, Trump's next act, COVID endgame scenarios & more
description:
Follow the crew:

https://twitter.com/chamath

https://linktr.ee/calacanis

https://twitter.com/DavidSacks

https://twitter.com/friedberg

Follow the pod:

https://twitter.com/theallinpod

https://linktr.ee/allinpodcast

Show Notes:

0:00 Besties reflect on the election night special, comparing Trump's case to Al Gore's in 2000

4:44 Was this election devastating for extreme-ism in politics?

12:13 Pfizer vaccine, withholding the vaccine from the political news cycle

21:54 COVID endgame scenarios

28:45 Public markets ripping, gridlock great for the economy

34:43 Analyzing exit polls, death of identity politics

50:57 Biggest loser of the election: Journalism

57:43 When will Trump concede? What will his next move be?

1:07:37 San Francisco's collapse - will it eventually go bankrupt?

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast crew discusses the 2020 US election, Pfizer's COVID vaccine, and the potential endgame scenarios for the pandemic. They also analyze the impact of the election on markets and the future of Trump's political career. The crew reflects on the state of journalism and the potential collapse of San Francisco.

takeaways.text: Election results may signal a decline in extreme-ism
Pfizer vaccine was withheld from the news cycle
Gridlock can be beneficial for the economy
Exit polls indicate a decline in identity politics
Journalism was the biggest loser in the election
San Francisco's economic collapse may lead to bankruptcy
COVID endgame scenarios are still uncertain

sentiment.notes: The crew's mood is mixed, reflecting on both the positive and negative aspects of the election results and the pandemic's impact on the economy.

notable_moments.description: Crew reflects on election night special
Discussion on when Trump will concede
Analysis of San Francisco's potential bankruptcy
